dashboard.title: Your admin dashboard
dashboard.summary: Track applications, sessions, and what needs your attention.

overview.title: Stat cards at a glance
overview.summary: The four cards at the top show total applications, pending (submitted and not yet reviewed), accepted, and rejected counts for the active session.
overview.details: Numbers update whenever an application status changes. Use them as a quick pulse check before diving into individual reviews.

needs_attention.title: Needs Attention list
needs_attention.summary: This list surfaces the applications that have been waiting longest without a decision — oldest submission date first.
needs_attention.details: Prioritizing the oldest applications helps ensure no applicant waits an unreasonable amount of time. Click any row to jump straight to that review.

sessions_carousel.title: Session capacity cards
sessions_carousel.summary: Each session card shows current enrollment versus total capacity. The bar turns yellow when a session reaches 80% full and red when it reaches 100%.
sessions_carousel.details: A red card means the session is at capacity. New applicants selecting that session will be waitlisted automatically.

recent_activity.title: Recent activity feed
recent_activity.summary: The activity feed shows the six most recently updated applications so you can quickly pick up where you left off.
recent_activity.details: Each entry shows the applicant name, camper name, and the last status change. Click the entry to open that application.

jump_to_review.title: Jumping straight to a review
jump_to_review.summary: Click any application card — in Needs Attention or Recent Activity — to go directly to that application's review page.
jump_to_review.details: You do not need to navigate to the Applications list first. Any card on the dashboard is a direct link.

inbox_badge.title: Inbox and notification badge
inbox_badge.summary: The bell icon in the navigation bar shows a badge with the count of unread inbox messages and pending system notifications.
inbox_badge.details: Click the bell to see a summary, or click Inbox in the sidebar to open the full message list.

oldest_first.question: What does "oldest first" mean for Needs Attention?
oldest_first.answer: Applications are sorted by submission date with the earliest date at the top. This surfaces the applicants who have been waiting the longest so they are reviewed first.

red_session.question: Why is a session card red?
red_session.answer: The card turns red when enrolled applicants equal the session's total capacity. No new approved applicants can be added without increasing the capacity limit or waitlisting.

overdue_pending.message: {{count}} pending application(s); the oldest has waited {{days}} day(s).

all_caught_up.message: No pending applications. You are caught up.

stat-cards.title: Session overview
stat-cards.body: These four cards show registered families, enrolled campers, active applications, and multi-camper families for the selected session. Use the session selector to switch between sessions.

needs-attention.title: Needs Attention
needs-attention.body: This list shows applications that have waited the longest without a decision — oldest first. Click any row to open that application directly.

sessions-carousel.title: Session capacity
sessions-carousel.body: Each session card shows enrollment versus capacity. The bar turns yellow at 80% and red at 100% — a full session automatically waitlists new applicants.

recent-activity.title: Recent activity
recent-activity.body: The feed shows the six most recently updated applications and inbox messages so you can pick up where you left off.

applicationReview.title: Reviewing an application
applicationReview.summary: Approve, reject, or request more documents from one place.

sections_overview.title: All 11 sections in one view
sections_overview.summary: The page shows every section of the application — camper info, emergency contacts, medical history, behavior, diet, equipment, medications, communication, legal consents, signature, and documents.
sections_overview.details: Click the pencil icon next to any field to edit it on behalf of the applicant. Changes are saved immediately and the applicant is not notified unless you send a message.

start_review.title: Starting a review
start_review.summary: Click "Start Review" to soft-claim this application. Other admins can see that you have started, which prevents duplicate review work.
start_review.details: Starting a review transitions the status from Submitted to Under Review. It is not a lock — another admin can still open and complete the review if needed.

request_documents.title: Requesting documents
request_documents.summary: Use the "Request Document" button to ask the applicant for a specific file. They receive a notification and an inbox message with your instructions.
request_documents.details: You can attach instructions when creating the request, such as specifying which pages of a form are needed. The request appears in the applicant's Documents page.

verify_documents.title: Verifying documents
verify_documents.summary: Inline Verify and Reject buttons appear next to each uploaded document. Click Verify to approve it, or Reject to open a reason modal.
verify_documents.details: The rejection reason is sent to the applicant so they know exactly what to fix before re-uploading. A document must be verified before it counts toward the required documents checklist.

approve.title: Approving the application
approve.summary: Click Approve when all required fields and documents are present. The "Ready" badge next to the approve button confirms completeness.
approve.details: Approving sets the application status to Approved, enrolls the camper in the selected session, and sends the applicant a notification. This action is significant — confirm all information is correct before approving.

reject_or_waitlist.title: Rejecting or waitlisting
reject_or_waitlist.summary: Reject is a final decision and notifies the applicant immediately. Waitlist holds the application — the camper may move up if a spot opens.
reject_or_waitlist.details: Both actions trigger an automatic inbox message to the applicant. If you reject, include a clear reason so the applicant understands what happened.

after_approve.question: What happens after I approve?
after_approve.answer: The application status changes to Approved, the camper is enrolled in the selected session, and the applicant receives a notification with next steps.

undo_approval.question: Can I undo an approval?
undo_approval.answer: Yes. Reversing an approval returns the application to Under Review status. Use this if you approved in error. The applicant is notified of the status change.

missing_document.question: What if a document is missing?
missing_document.answer: Use "Request Document" to ask the applicant for the specific file. They receive an inbox message with your instructions and a link to upload directly to that request.

claim_reminder.message: Click "Start Review" to claim this application and let other admins know you are reviewing it.

items_missing.message: {{count}} item(s) still missing before this application is ready to approve.

ready_to_decide.message: Everything looks complete. Ready to Approve, Reject, or Waitlist.

header-stat-cards.title: Application at a glance
header-stat-cards.body: The header shows the camper name, current status badge, application number, submission date, and position in the review queue.

sections-list.title: All 11 sections
sections-list.body: Every section of the application is shown here — camper info, emergency contacts, medical history, behavior, diet, equipment, medications, communication, consents, signature, and documents. Click the pencil icon to edit any section.

review-panel.title: Reviewer tools
review-panel.body: This panel is the control center for every review action. Add internal notes, then use the status buttons below to record your decision.

decision-buttons.title: Making a decision
decision-buttons.body: Click "Start Review" to claim the application. Once you are satisfied with what you see, use Approve, Reject, or Waitlist to record your final decision.

request-document.title: Request a document
request-document.body: Use this panel to ask the applicant for a specific document. They receive an inbox notification with your instructions and can upload directly to the request.

documentQueue.title: Document Control Center
documentQueue.summary: Track all document requests, verify uploads, and resolve overdue items.

metric_cards.title: Metric cards
metric_cards.summary: The cards at the top show how many document requests are in each state: awaiting upload, uploaded, under review, approved, rejected, overdue, and completed.
metric_cards.details: Use the metric cards to spot where work is piling up. A high overdue count usually means applicants need a reminder or a deadline extension.

review_queue.title: Review Queue tab
review_queue.summary: Switch to the Review Queue tab to see uploads that are waiting for your review. Click a row to verify or reject the document.
review_queue.details: Documents in the review queue have been submitted by applicants and are waiting for an admin to look at them. Work through this list regularly to avoid delays.

create_request.title: Creating a document request
create_request.summary: Click "Create Request" to ask a specific applicant for a document. You can add instructions and a due date.
create_request.details: The applicant receives a notification and an inbox message. The request shows up in their Documents page with your instructions.

overdue.title: Handling overdue items
overdue.summary: Overdue requests either need a deadline extension or a reminder to the applicant. Do not leave overdue items stale — they block the review process.
overdue.details: An overdue item means the applicant missed the due date. Send a reminder if they have not responded, or extend the deadline if more time is reasonable.

reminder_extend.title: Remind and Extend actions
reminder_extend.summary: "Remind" sends the applicant a notification that their document request is still outstanding. "Extend" pushes the due date forward.
reminder_extend.details: Use Remind first. If the applicant responds that they need more time, use Extend to update the deadline. Both actions are logged in the request history.

awaiting_vs_under_review.question: What is the difference between "awaiting upload" and "under review"?
awaiting_vs_under_review.answer: Awaiting upload means the applicant has not uploaded anything yet. Under review means they uploaded a file and it is waiting for you to verify or reject it.

cancel_request.question: Can I cancel a document request?
cancel_request.answer: Yes. Find the request in the list and click the cancel button on its row. The applicant is notified that the request has been cancelled.

overdue_count.message: {{count}} document request(s) are overdue and need attention.

uploaded_waiting.message: {{count}} document(s) have been uploaded and are waiting for review.

metric-cards.title: Status at a glance
metric-cards.body: Each card shows how many document requests are in that state. Click a card to filter the table below to that status instantly.

tabs.title: Two tabs, two views
tabs.body: "Document Requests" shows admin-initiated requests and their lifecycle. "Uploaded Documents" shows all files applicants have submitted for review.

requests-table.title: Requests table
requests-table.body: Each row shows the applicant, document type, status, and due date. Use the action buttons on a row to approve, reject, remind, or extend the deadline.

create-request.title: Create a request
create-request.body: Click here to ask a specific applicant for a document. You can add instructions and a due date — the applicant receives an inbox notification immediately.

reports.title: Reports
reports.summary: View key metrics and download CSV reports for your records.

stat_cards.title: Top-level stats
stat_cards.summary: The cards at the top show total campers, accepted count, rejected count, and acceptance rate for the current selection.
stat_cards.details: These numbers update when you change the session filter. Use them as a quick summary before exporting a detailed CSV.

charts.title: Charts
charts.summary: The page includes a bar chart of applications by status, a pie chart showing acceptance distribution, a line chart of applications over time, and horizontal bars for enrollment per session.
charts.details: Charts are visual aids. For exact numbers, generate a CSV export — the charts round figures for readability.

export_csv.title: Exporting a CSV
export_csv.summary: Five report types are available to download: All Applications, Accepted Only, Rejected Only, Mailing Labels, and ID Labels.
export_csv.details: Click the export button for the report type you need. The CSV downloads to your browser's default downloads folder. You can open it in any spreadsheet application.

mailing_labels.title: Mailing Labels report
mailing_labels.summary: The Mailing Labels CSV formats applicant names and mailing addresses for printing on standard adhesive label sheets.
mailing_labels.details: The columns match common label-sheet templates (for example, Avery 5160). Import the CSV into your label software and print.

id_labels.title: ID Labels report
id_labels.summary: The ID Labels CSV includes each camper's name and their emergency contact information for printing camp ID badges.
id_labels.details: Review the emergency contact fields on each application before printing ID labels to make sure the information is current.

real_time.question: Are reports updated in real time?
real_time.answer: Yes. Each CSV export reflects the live database at the moment you generate it. Refresh the page or click the export button again to get the latest data.

csv_location.question: Where do CSV files go?
csv_location.answer: Files download to your browser's default downloads folder. Check your operating system's Downloads folder or the downloads section of your browser.

export_tip.message: Generate a report by clicking one of the export buttons below the charts.

stat-cards.title: Key numbers
stat-cards.body: These cards summarize total campers, accepted count, rejected count, and acceptance rate across all sessions.

charts-grid.title: Visual analytics
charts-grid.body: Four charts: applications by status, acceptance rate donut, monthly submission trend, and enrollment per session. Hover over any data point for exact figures.

export-buttons.title: Export reports
export-buttons.body: Click any button to download a CSV. Five types are available: All Applications, Accepted, Rejected, Mailing Labels, and ID Labels. Files save to your browser's downloads folder.

sessions.title: Camp sessions
sessions.summary: Create, edit, and monitor camp session capacity.

session_cards.title: Session cards
session_cards.summary: Each card shows a session name, date range, total capacity, and current enrollment count.
session_cards.details: Sessions are listed in date order, earliest first. Click a card to open the session detail page with enrollment metrics and a full applicant list.

create_session.title: Creating a session
create_session.summary: Click "+ Create Session" to add a new camp session. Fill in the name, start and end dates, and capacity.
create_session.details: Sessions are immediately visible to applicants on the application form once created. Set a realistic capacity — you can adjust it later.

capacity_warning.title: Capacity warnings
capacity_warning.summary: A session's enrollment bar turns yellow when it reaches 80% of capacity and red when it reaches 100%. Watch for full sessions to avoid overbooking.
capacity_warning.details: At 100%, new applicants selecting that session will be waitlisted automatically. You can increase the capacity limit to accept more applicants.

archive_vs_delete.title: Archive versus delete
archive_vs_delete.summary: Archive hides a session from active lists but keeps all its data and enrollments intact. Delete is permanent and cannot be undone.
archive_vs_delete.details: Use Archive for past sessions you no longer need to appear in active dropdowns. Only delete a session that was created in error and has no enrollments.

view_dashboard.title: Session dashboard
view_dashboard.summary: Click "View Dashboard" on a session card to see session-scoped metrics: applications by status, enrollment progress, and recent activity for that session only.
view_dashboard.details: The session dashboard is useful when you are running multiple sessions simultaneously and want to focus on one at a time.

archive_effect.question: What does archiving a session do?
archive_effect.answer: Archiving hides the session from active lists and the applicant-facing session selector. All enrolled applicants and application data are preserved. You can restore a session at any time.

capacity_after_enrollment.question: Can I change capacity after enrollment starts?
capacity_after_enrollment.answer: Yes. You can increase or decrease the capacity at any time. Existing enrolled applicants are not affected — they keep their spots even if you reduce the capacity below the current enrolled count.

sessions_full.message: {{count}} session(s) are at capacity. New applicants will be waitlisted for those sessions.

sessions_near_capacity.message: {{count}} session(s) are approaching capacity (80% or more full).
